text.source_text: इतश्च परमेश्वर एव वैश्वानरः ; यस्मात्परमेश्वरस्यैव ‘ अग्निरास्यं द्यौर्मूर्धा ’ इतीदृशं त्रैलोक्यात्मकं रूपं स्मर्यते — ‘ यस्याग्निरास्यं द्यौर्मूर्धा खं नाभिश्चरणौ क्षितिः । सूर्यश्चक्षुर्दिशः श्रोत्रं तस्मै लोकात्मने नमः ’ इति । एतत्स्मर्यमाणं रूपं मूलभूतां श्रुतिमनुमापयदस्य वैश्वानरशब्दस्य परमेश्वरपरत्वे अनुमानं लिङ्गं गमकं स्यादित्यर्थः । इतिशब्दो हेत्वर्थे — यस्मादिदं गमकम् , तस्मादपि वैश्वानरः परमात्मैवेत्यर्थः । यद्यपि स्तुति रि यम् — ‘ तस्मै लोकात्मने नमः ’ इति , तथापि स्तुतित्वमपि नासति मूलभूते वेदवाक्ये सम्यक् ईदृशेन रूपेण सम्भवति । ‘ द्यां मूर्धानं यस्य विप्रा वदन्ति खं वै नाभिं चन्द्रसूर्यौ च नेत्रे । दिशः श्रोत्रे विद्धि पादौ क्षितिं च सोऽचिन्त्यात्मा सर्वभूतप्रणेता ’ इत्येवंजातीयका च स्मृतिरिहोदाहर्तव्या ॥ २५ ॥
text.transliteration_iast: itaśca parameśvara eva vaiśvānaraḥ ; yasmātparameśvarasyaiva ‘ agnirāsyaṃ dyaurmūrdhā ’ itīdṛśaṃ trailokyātmakaṃ rūpaṃ smaryate — ‘ yasyāgnirāsyaṃ dyaurmūrdhā khaṃ nābhiścaraṇau kṣitiḥ | sūryaścakṣurdiśaḥ śrotraṃ tasmai lokātmane namaḥ ’ iti | etatsmaryamāṇaṃ rūpaṃ mūlabhūtāṃ śrutimanumāpayadasya vaiśvānaraśabdasya parameśvaraparatve anumānaṃ liṅgaṃ gamakaṃ syādityarthaḥ | itiśabdo hetvarthe — yasmādidaṃ gamakam , tasmādapi vaiśvānaraḥ paramātmaivetyarthaḥ | yadyapi stuti ri yam — ‘ tasmai lokātmane namaḥ ’ iti , tathāpi stutitvamapi nāsati mūlabhūte vedavākye samyak īdṛśena rūpeṇa sambhavati | ‘ dyāṃ mūrdhānaṃ yasya viprā vadanti khaṃ vai nābhiṃ candrasūryau ca netre | diśaḥ śrotre viddhi pādau kṣitiṃ ca so'cintyātmā sarvabhūtapraṇetā ’ ityevaṃjātīyakā ca smṛtirihodāhartavyā || 25 ||

translation.literal_translation: For this reason too, Vaishvanara is only the Supreme Lord: because this very three-world form - 'fire is His mouth, heaven His head' - is remembered only of the Supreme. Smrti says: 'whose mouth is fire, heaven the head, space the navel, earth the feet, sun the eye, directions the ear - to that world-self, reverence.' This remembered form, by pointing back to an underlying sruti-root, serves as inferential indicator that the term Vaishvanara here refers to the Supreme Lord. The word 'iti' here carries causal force: since this is an indicator, therefore too Vaishvanara is the Supreme Self. Though the cited text is eulogistic ('to that world-self, reverence'), even such praise would not arise correctly in this exact form without a real Vedic root statement. A similar smrti should also be cited: 'whose head the sages call heaven, whose navel space, whose eyes moon and sun, whose ears the directions, whose feet earth - that inconceivable self, leader of all beings.' (25)
translation.technical_translation: Bhashya on 1.2.25 adduces smrti as anumana-linga for Vaisvanara=Brahman, arguing remembered cosmic-body formulas presuppose an original sruti source.
translation.interpretive_note: Smrti operates as corroborative inferential evidence, not independent scriptural replacement.

lexical_notes.contextual_sense: Smrti cosmic-body verse is treated as a valid linga supporting Vaisvanara=Brahman.
Universal form-ascription aligns with Brahmanic cosmological scope.
Smrti epithet reinforcing totalized, non-local referent identity.

lexical_notes.gloss: principle that valid Smrti rests on Sruti source
lexical_notes.contextual_sense: Justifies importing Smrti as secondary evidence in Vedantic referent determination.
Net function of Smrti citation: corroborative reinforcement of siddhanta.

uncertainty_notes: Curated in contiguous S02 pass with stable cadence and inferential-tag schema.
A later philological pass should revisit vaisvanara lexical-rhetorical pivots and drsti-upadesa framing in records 1.2.063-1.2.070.
